task_identity.title: Create public surface inventory and claim-tier ledger
task_identity.source_scope: exports, CLI, IPC, schemas, docs, packages, GitHub metadata

preconditions: HEAD equals 9cf56e149a105026b072c9073d7e87b93103966e or an approved updated audit cut exists.
Every in-scope tracked/generated file is present in FILE_REVIEW_LEDGER.csv and assigned to a named reviewer.
The current behavior, public claim tier, assumptions, affected consumers, and rollback are recorded before editing.
procedure: Read every in-scope file line by line, including tests, examples, generated output, workflow, documentation, and relevant Git history; record blob and SHA-256 identities.
Write a current-behavior table covering inputs, validation, state, transitions, outputs, errors, resources, time, identity, persistence, concurrency, and public claims.
Enumerate all plausible semantic interpretations and construct at least one counterexample or misuse for each rejected interpretation.
Write normative SHALL/SHALL NOT requirements with stable IDs and exact accepted/rejected cases; obtain lead approval before implementation.
Implement the smallest coherent change and delete, quarantine, or version any contradictory legacy/default/fallback path.
Add positive, malformed, empty, min/max, non-finite, duplicate, stale, reordered, replayed, future, capacity, cancellation, restart, and migration tests as applicable.
Add property/metamorphic, differential/oracle, fuzz, mutation-sensitive, concurrency, resource, and integration tests appropriate to the claim tier.
Generate evidence from a clean checkout with exact tools, commands, configs, seeds, data/models, platform/hardware, raw outputs, exit status, and checksums.
Update schemas, API snapshots, migration, examples, README/docs, claim/evidence ledger, support matrix, and exact downstream pins.
Have an independent reviewer reproduce the decisive evidence and review every changed line plus relevant unchanged context.
Run the subsystem gate, complete wave gate, full locked CI, and every affected downstream conformance job.
Close only after all twenty lenses are resolved with evidence; otherwise remove/narrow the claim and leave the task OPEN.
mandatory_counterfactuals: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples
required_evidence: positive and negative vectors or fixtures
coverage, fuzz/mutation or model results where applicable
handoff_task_contract.completion_rule: No prose-only closure. Every acceptance condition and evidence item must pass on the exact candidate; otherwise mark NOT_CLAIMED or NO_GO.

twenty_lenses.name: Claims and scope
twenty_lenses.question: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.

twenty_lenses.name: First-principles semantics
twenty_lenses.question: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?

twenty_lenses.name: Mathematics and statistics
twenty_lenses.question: Are definitions, assumptions, estimands, finite-sample behavior, multiplicity, uncertainty, calibration, and failure regions correct and explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Type and state integrity
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid combinations be constructed through safe APIs, deserialization, FFI, feature flags, defaults, or migration?

twenty_lenses.name: Time, ordering, and replay
twenty_lenses.question: Are clock domains, freshness, deadlines, sessions, epochs, generations, sequence numbers, duplicates, restart, and rollover unambiguous?

twenty_lenses.name: Identity and provenance
twenty_lenses.question: Can every result, command, model, dataset, scene, schema, configuration, build, and evidence artifact be traced immutably?

twenty_lenses.name: Authentication and cryptography
twenty_lenses.question: Are canonical bytes, domain separation, algorithms, identities, rotation, revocation, downgrade resistance, and failure semantics complete?

twenty_lenses.name: Authority and safety
twenty_lenses.question: Who may observe, advise, intend, authorize, publish, apply, and confirm? Can any fallback or optional component widen authority?

twenty_lenses.name: Hostile inputs and parsers
twenty_lenses.question: What happens with duplicates, unknown fields, unsafe integers, NaN/Inf, Unicode ambiguity, malformed nesting, path tricks, and contradictory metadata?

twenty_lenses.name: Resource and denial-of-service bounds
twenty_lenses.question: Are bytes, allocations, dimensions, states, queues, retries, threads, log volume, GPU/CPU work, and serialization bounded before expensive work?

twenty_lenses.name: Concurrency and lifecycle
twenty_lenses.question: Are initialization, callback ownership, races, cancellation, selected events, cleanup, crash consistency, and shutdown fully specified?

twenty_lenses.name: Determinism and reproducibility
twenty_lenses.question: Can independent clean machines reproduce code generation, builds, fixtures, simulations, proofs, benchmarks, evidence, and archives?

twenty_lenses.name: API, FFI, and SemVer
twenty_lenses.question: Is the stable surface minimal and are panic, ownership, allocation, thread safety, features, MSRV/runtime, and compatibility explicit?

twenty_lenses.name: Schema, wire, and language parity
twenty_lenses.question: Is there one normative semantic source and do all languages/encodings accept and reject the same corpus?

twenty_lenses.name: Configuration and deployment
twenty_lenses.question: Can invalid profiles start? Are paths, secrets, permissions, ACLs, certificates, environments, startup order, and rollback safe?

twenty_lenses.name: Observability and forensics
twenty_lenses.question: Can disabled, unavailable, stale, incompatible, insufficient, anomalous, denied, and internal-fault states be distinguished without leaking secrets?

twenty_lenses.name: Verification and evidence quality
twenty_lenses.question: Does each claim have the right proof: theorem, model, property, fuzz, mutation, clean-room, real-router, statistical, or physical evidence?

twenty_lenses.name: Ecosystem composition
twenty_lenses.question: Are pid-rs, NCP, Engram, Prisoma, Crebain, Galadriel, Haldir, ROS, and external authority relationships explicit and acyclic?

twenty_lenses.name: Human factors and governance
twenty_lenses.question: Are naming, docs, examples, runbooks, review roles, incident response, support, deprecation, and withdrawal usable under stress?

twenty_lenses.name: Counterfactual and quirky-case review
twenty_lenses.question: How would a malicious peer, rushed operator, odd-but-simple dataset, future maintainer, or AI-generated patch falsify the intended claim?

affected_surface_inventory.rationale: Machine view of every claim row, exact evidence text, active-state disposition, and non-expansive evidence tier.
Exact signed-F and planned-I assignment overlay that closes every candidate path without claiming qualification review early.
Bounded, paginated, status-aware GitHub repository metadata captured between signed F and signed I.
Exact bridge from the activated CH-T002 ledger and lifecycle into the complete CH-T003 candidate partition.
Complete signed-F file inventory plus compiler-resolved Rust exports and exact package, CLI, IPC, schema, documentation, archive, build, and deployment surfaces.
Bounded word-boundary scan of the exact handoff vocabulary plus extended claim language over tracked text, archive text, CLI output, and GitHub description.

external_consumers: Repository readers and downstream documentation tooling
affected_surface_inventory.rationale: One claim row is repaired and narrowed to repository verification while higher evidence tiers remain excluded.
Deterministic builder for all six generated CH-T003 records.
Positive, hostile-input, metamorphic, differential, mutation, and exact-bound tests for the product tool.

normative_controls.statement: The signed F freeze SHALL bind CH-T003 epoch 1, release 0.9.0, Sepehr Mahmoudian, and a null persistent identifier.
The signed F freeze SHALL declare exactly the nine sorted implementation paths and the same exact verification-trigger path set.
The public-surface inventory SHALL include every regular Git blob at the signed F tree with exactly one in-scope or closed-exclusion disposition.
Every signed-F public inventory record SHALL bind exact Git mode, object type, object id, SHA-256, byte count, line count, content kind, classifications, and claim identifiers.

Typed surfaces SHALL inventory compiler-resolved Rust exports for every library and frozen feature and target profile, all Cargo targets, all command-line entry points plus the declared critical runtime observations, IPC routes, schemas, documentation, archives, packages, build inputs, deployment inputs, and release records.
The claim-tier ledger SHALL parse every unique claim row and SHALL bind the exact statement and evidence hashes without accepting a missing or duplicate status.
The claim-tier ledger SHALL use only IMPLEMENTED, VERIFIED, VALIDATED, DEPLOYMENT_QUALIFIED, FIELD_VALIDATED, and NOT_CLAIMED; no CH-T003 tier SHALL create release authority.
The implementation SHALL change exactly one claim row, SHALL retain its PROVEN status, and SHALL narrow CL-PUBLICATION-EVIDENCE-PRIMITIVE-01 to VERIFIED without changing non-row claim-ledger bytes.

The claim-language record SHALL preserve the exact handoff word-boundary baseline and add tracked source, archive member, CLI output, package, and GitHub-description coverage with source and line digests.

The GitHub metadata record SHALL retain bounded paginated captures with API version and status. It SHALL retain validators and raw body identity only for non-sensitive responses. It SHALL retain canonical sanitized-document identity for sensitive responses. It SHALL report repository, branch, protection, workflow, rule, release, tag, community, and security state. GitHub Packages are not observed.
The review overlay and ledger composition SHALL bind the exact activated CH-T002 lifecycle and prove a disjoint, duplicate-free, gap-free partition of all 434 planned I-tree regular blobs.
All generated JSON SHALL be duplicate-free, finite, bounded, canonical UTF-8 JSON with one trailing newline.

The product and registered verifiers SHALL reject missing, extra, reordered, duplicated, stale, unsafe, or hash-mismatched paths, archive members, exports, targets, routes, schemas, claims, and GitHub pages.
The claim partition SHALL remain exact across active, non-claimed, and removed sets; CH-T003 SHALL add only the one frozen narrowed-claim disposition.

Every retained composition and metadata record SHALL distinguish source declarations, compiler verification, local runtime observations, live bindings, deployment qualification, later external state, and independent-person review.
CH-T003 SHALL remain NO_GO and SHALL authorize no tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, archive, deployment, or publication.
Every binary and archive SHALL be explicitly bounded and safely classified; undecodable, encrypted, duplicate, traversing, linked, nested, oversized, or ambiguous content SHALL fail closed.
Every claim and surface identifier SHALL be sorted, unique, and bidirectionally reconciled; a dangling claim-to-surface or surface-to-claim reference SHALL fail closed.
The F outcome SHALL narrow exactly CL-PUBLICATION-EVIDENCE-PRIMITIVE-01 and SHALL keep every prior active, removed, and non-claimed claim in its prior partition.
The registered verifier SHALL accept only the adjacent signed F, I, C, and D subjects and SHALL emit one exact canonical PASS record.

mandatory_counterfactuals.statement: correct version string with wrong contract/algorithm digest
clean synthetic result under nonrepresentative distribution
timeout or capacity exhaustion followed by convenience fallback
feature unification activating a privileged/experimental path
partial migration accepted through default values
crash between decision/output and durable evidence
simple but odd input unlikely to resemble training examples

combined_attack_matrix.statement: valid signature plus stale epoch or wrong route
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: authenticated peer plus oversized payload and expensive logging
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.
The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: green unit tests plus divergent generated binding
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: nominal statistic plus missing lifecycle/projection evidence
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T003 records offline inventory and claim scope and does not perform this runtime operation.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T003 implementation path begins performing the described runtime or statistical operation.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: ALLOW then crash before/after publication and restart
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T003 records offline inventory and claim scope and does not perform this runtime operation.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T003 implementation path begins performing the described runtime or statistical operation.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: matching package version plus wrong algorithm/contract digest
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: timeout or optional-component loss plus convenience fallback
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: correct local ACL file plus wrong real router
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T003 records offline inventory and claim scope and does not perform this runtime operation.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T003 implementation path begins performing the described runtime or statistical operation.

combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: canonical payload plus noncanonical signing bytes
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.
The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.
The exact inventory, lifecycle, and evidence bindings reject this combined mismatch.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: clock rollback plus apparently valid TTL/lease
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T003 records offline inventory and claim scope and does not perform this runtime operation.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T003 implementation path begins performing the described runtime or statistical operation.

combined_attack_matrix.statement: cancellation request plus already-selected event and duplicate effect
combined_attack_matrix.rationale: CH-T003 records offline inventory and claim scope and does not perform this runtime operation.
combined_attack_matrix.falsifier: A CH-T003 implementation path begins performing the described runtime or statistical operation.

handoff_command_mapping.rationale: The exact prior signed task artifact is retained and re-bound by CH-T003 composition evidence.
The exact prior signed task artifact is retained and re-bound by CH-T003 composition evidence.
The exact prior signed task artifact is retained and re-bound by CH-T003 composition evidence.
The replacement commands scan every conservative public surface, verify typed declarations and claim tiers, and run independent lifecycle checks.

threat_model.preconditions: ability to alter one candidate input or retained artifact
threat_model.sequence: omit one public file while preserving category totals
threat_model.trust_boundary: exact signed Git objects and retained raw evidence versus derived inventory claims
threat_model.observable_symptoms: path, digest, count, state, chronology, or authority mismatch
threat_model.worst_consequence: a reader accepts a public-surface or claim tier that the exact evidence does not support
threat_model.recovery: stop qualification, restore the exact prior activated state, correct the record, and start a new signed epoch if F changed
threat_model.residual_risk: deterministic inventory proves bounded repository facts and does not establish later external state or operational fitness
threat_model.claim_impact: the task remains NO_GO and the affected inventory assertion fails closed

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: This surface can overstate scope unless it is bound to exact lifecycle objects and non-expansive claim semantics.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Use the exact claim row and active-state partition.
non_proofs: inventory verification is not release qualification, named-human review, deployment approval, or field validation

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: This surface can overstate scope unless it is bound to exact lifecycle objects and non-expansive claim semantics.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Retain exact raw API body identity (byte count and SHA-256) for non-sensitive responses, canonical sanitized documents for sensitive responses, and normalized zero tag/release counts.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Trust an uncaptured dashboard view or later cache.
non_proofs: inventory verification is not release qualification, named-human review, deployment approval, or field validation

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: This surface can overstate scope unless it is bound to exact lifecycle objects and non-expansive claim semantics.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Inventory every conservative public file and typed declaration at signed I.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Inventory only obvious Rust exports or documentation.
non_proofs: inventory verification is not release qualification, named-human review, deployment approval, or field validation

misuse_resistant_interfaces.justification: This surface can overstate scope unless it is bound to exact lifecycle objects and non-expansive claim semantics.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.correct_example: Run only with exact signed F, I, C, D, and current commits.
misuse_resistant_interfaces.wrong_example: Run against worktree bytes or infer release authority from PASS.
non_proofs: inventory verification is not release qualification, named-human review, deployment approval, or field validation

lens_questions.name: Claims and scope
lens_questions.question: Can a reader infer more than the exact evidence tier supports? Separate implemented, verified, validated, deployment-qualified, field-validated, and not-claimed.

lens_questions.name: First-principles semantics
lens_questions.question: Are inputs, state, transitions, outputs, invariants, units, and failure states explicit rather than implied by names?

limitations: Automated technical review does not constitute named-human or external review.
Compiler-resolved Rust API output is bound to cargo-public-api 0.52.0 and Rust 1.96.0; the external API tool is version-pinned for this evidence but is not a workspace dependency.
GitHub metadata is an exact bounded capture; the I record does not prove later GitHub state, and C must bind hosted main to the exact I commit.
No release, deployment, publication, tag, GitHub release, DOI, Zenodo record, or archive authority is granted.
The claim-row change narrows one repository claim to VERIFIED and explicitly excludes VALIDATED, DEPLOYMENT_QUALIFIED, FIELD_VALIDATED, and release-qualified interpretations.
The package version 0.1.0-experimental, author Sepahead, and publish=false disagree with the planned 0.9.0 author metadata and remain visible for successor correction.
